title: Add version command to pypodman
body:
pypodman does not currently support the version command.  We want to
have as close to the same functionality between podman and pypodman,
so adding this command.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

I'm not sure where, but git looks to be complaining about some whitespace errors.

```
GIT_CHECK_EXCLUDE="./vendor" /go/bin/git-validation -v -run DCO,short-subject,dangling-whitespace -range 921ccac10c47e0865ec5e4ba00ebb69a03d89473..HEAD
 * b4cbca21 "Add version command to pypodman" ... FAIL
  - FAIL - has whitespace errors. See `git show --check b4cbca21cb0609ca5446c1320e49a705d53d6f3f`.
  - PASS - has a valid DCO
  - PASS - commit subject is 72 characters or less! *yay*
```
LGTM, but would like a head nod from @jwhonce
@TomSweeneyRedHat Validate found another issue and the whitespace problem was the new program ended in a blank line.
